title: Thyroid result review

investigations.text: Specifies clinician review of TSH, free T4, and T3 over time and notes adherence or symptoms as reasons for earlier testing.
investigations.rationale: Relevant to recording already reviewed TSH, FT4 and T3 trends in treatment context without interpreting values or setting timing.
investigations.evidence_extract: Specifies clinician review of TSH, free T4, and T3 over time and notes adherence or symptoms as reasons for earlier testing.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Laboratory Monitoring of Various Chronic Conditions/Diseases – 21

exact_section.heading: Appendix 2 — antithyroid and thyroxine therapy monitoring

investigations.text: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.
investigations.rationale: Relevant to clinician-entered indication, TSH, FT4, FT3 and biotin context without assigning thyroid status.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.2 — suspected dysfunction testing and antibodies

treatment_and_management.text: Includes hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and hypercholesterolaemia/hyperlipidaemia among the listed chronic conditions.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Relevant only when a listed established thyroid condition is clinician entered.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Includes hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and hypercholesterolaemia/hyperlipidaemia among the listed chronic conditions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Laboratory Monitoring of Various Chronic Conditions/Diseases – 21

exact_section.heading: 5.1 Chronic conditions that could be managed virtually

medication.text: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH and FT4 monitoring, persistent-symptom review, and clinician-led follow-up for confirmed primary hypothyroidism without mapping a dose or autonomous treatment change.
medication.rationale: Relevant only to clinician-led monitoring of established primary hypothyroidism.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH and FT4 monitoring, persistent-symptom review, and clinician-led follow-up for confirmed primary hypothyroidism without mapping a dose or autonomous treatment change.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.6 — follow-up and monitoring of primary hypothyroidism

referral.text: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH, FT4, and FT3 monitoring, treatment-context review, and clinician-led follow-up or specialist advice without mapping medicine doses or autonomous treatment.
referral.rationale: Relevant only to clinician-led monitoring of established hyperthyroidism.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH, FT4, and FT3 monitoring, treatment-context review, and clinician-led follow-up or specialist advice without mapping medicine doses or autonomous treatment.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.8.5 — follow-up and monitoring of hyperthyroidism

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Analyte, value, unit, date, reference interval, indication, diagnosis, symptoms, pregnancy, medicine, adherence and prior trend are absent.
No thyroid diagnosis, interpretation, test order, monitoring interval, medicine or dose change, referral or urgency is generated.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
